description_long: This object is a vertical stack of large, roughly rectangular stone blocks, each with slightly rounded edges and subtle surface imperfections. The blocks are arranged in a somewhat irregular column, with some stones protruding more than others, giving the structure a rugged, hand-built appearance. The surface of each block is matte and displays minor pitting and discoloration, suggesting natural stone or concrete. The coloration is a consistent light gray, with some darker spots and faint weathering marks. The overall form is tall and narrow, with the blocks closely fitted but not mortared, creating visible seams between them. This structure resembles a barrier or obstacle commonly found in urban or landscaped environments.
description: A tall, irregularly stacked column of light gray stone blocks with rounded edges and a rugged, weathered surface.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full height of the stacked blocks, highlighting the irregular arrangement and visible seams.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the narrow profile and the protrusion of some blocks, emphasizing the uneven stacking.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front but with subtle differences in block alignment and surface marks.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays the staggered block arrangement and the depth of the seams between stones.
attribute_location_context: urban park or landscaped public space
attribute_weathering_pattern: Minor surface pitting and discoloration are visible, with some darker spots suggesting light weathering but no significant erosion.
